title: INNOVATIVE THINKING, INFINITE HUMOR: HUMOR RESEARCH OF LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS THROUGH STRUCTURED THOUGHT LEAPS
venue: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2025
research_questions: Can injecting and augmenting external knowledge into humorous conversation data mitigate sparse knowledge-graph paths and improve multi-hop humor reasoning?
Can judgment-oriented supervised fine-tuning plus Direct Preference Optimization with GPT-4o-extracted rationales improve LLM humor judgment and generation?
How does LoL compare with GPT-4o, Qwen, LLaMA3, CLoT, QwQ, and other baselines on Chinese and English humor judgment tasks and humor generation preference tests?

datasets_used.name: Ruozhiba dataset
datasets_used.size: first 200 samples from the validation subset for the user study

methods_proposed.name: LoL
methods_proposed.summary: A two-stage framework for humor reasoning that first trains judgment and understanding abilities with supervised fine-tuning, then improves generation through Direct Preference Optimization with rationales.

methods_proposed.name: Automatic Instruction Expansion (AIE)
methods_proposed.summary: A three-agent instruction-evolution system that uses a generator, imaginator, and selector/critic to enrich seed humorous conversations with broader and deeper contextual knowledge while controlling over-divergence from the original joke.

methods_proposed.name: Judgement-oriented instruction templates
methods_proposed.summary: Human-designed templates train selection, ranking, answer rewriting, and teacher-student judgment skills from question-answer data with human-voting annotations.

methods_proposed.name: Teacher-Student Prompt Loop
methods_proposed.summary: Two GPT-4o agents simulate trial-and-error, reflection, and backtracking: a teacher gives feedback and hints while a student revises answers until the response aligns with the ideal humorous answer or reaches a step limit.

methods_proposed.name: Guided Explorative Self-Improvement Tuning (GESIT)
methods_proposed.summary: An online DPO training procedure where the SFT model is copied into a frozen judge and a trainable policy; sampled response pairs are judged, rationales are generated by GPT-4o, and the augmented preference data are mixed back into training.

methods_proposed.name: LoL inference step
methods_proposed.summary: At inference time, the model generates multiple candidate responses from sampled prompts and then uses its own judgment ability to select the funniest answer.

evaluation_methods.detail: Top-1 accuracy / acc% on single-choice humor judgment tasks: 2T1, 2T1(hard), 3T1, and 4T1.
Human-voting annotations and Likes from humor games and communities are used to construct positive/negative pairs and choice-question labels.
Divergent Association Task (DAT) scores are computed with Chinese Word Vectors to evaluate associative generalization and divergent thinking.
User-preference study over four LLMs on 200 text-based questions; the paper reports 15 valid questionnaires and 3000 votes.
Three experts assessed logical coherence of responses on 200 examples from GPT-4o, GPT-4, and QWEN2-57B.

headline_results.finding: On diverse English benchmarks, LoL’s average accuracy increased over LLAMA3-70B and GPT-4o.
On diverse Chinese benchmarks, LoL’s average accuracy increased over GPT-4o.
On English choice questions, OURS-32B achieved the best score on five of six columns; GPT-4o was best only on SemEval 2021 2T1(hard).
headline_results.dataset: HaHackathon (SemEval-2021 Task 7); Humicroedit + FunLines (SemEval-2020 Task 7); Oogiri-GO

headline_results.finding: On Chinese choice questions, OURS-32B was best on both reported benchmark tasks.
On the ruozhiba 2T1 validation, LoL achieved the top score, ahead of QwQ and GPT-4o.
In ablations, ALL+AIE(DIET) substantially improved over QWEN1.5-32B on Oogiri-GO-en and SemEval 2021 3T1.

key_findings: LoL improves humor judgment on nearly all reported English and Chinese choice-question benchmarks, with especially large gains on Chinese 2T1 tasks.
Judgment-oriented data construction, especially teacher-student feedback and AIE, is presented as important for training models to distinguish humorous from non-humorous answers.
The paper reports that AIE improves divergent associative thinking in DAT validation and that LoL has the highest stabilized DAT score among compared systems.
Guided Explorative Self-Improvement Tuning is reported to strengthen the logical coherence of generated answers in expert evaluation.
In the human preference study, users were reported to have a strong inclination to select LoL outputs as the most creative and humorous among LoL, GPT-4o, QWEN1.5-32B, and CLoT.
limitations: The paper states that humor is subjective and that a unified score may contain significant noise from topics and background.
The failure-case analysis says the level of creativity is uneven because creativity in the training samples varies significantly.
Some human-preferred responses beat LoL in the failure cases, showing that LoL’s outputs are not always the most preferred for all users.
The ablation discussion notes that using AIE only on Oogiri-GO-en caused a slight decrease in some performance, possibly due to overfitting to divergence of thought in that dataset.
The text reports human preference trends but the transcribed paper text does not provide exact numeric win-rate values from Figure 6(b) or Figure 6(c).
